ops.title: tilth CLI lane misclassifies existing read paths (spaces/extensionless) as searches
ops.description: Found by the fail-fast-read refuter pass (reproduced empirically): an EXISTING regular file whose path contains spaces (e.g. /tmp/.../file space.txt) or is a bare extensionless name gets classified by tilth v0.9.0's polymorphic positional as a search query/concept — `ccx code read "$path" --full` exits 0 with a phantom '# Search: … — 0 matches' even though os.Stat succeeded in ResolveReadPath. CLI lane only; proxy/exec use tilth's MCP read handler with an explicit path param and are immune. Candidate fixes: route CLI OpRead through an unambiguous tilth surface, or an upstream tilth flag to force path mode (no such flag known today).

ops.text: ccx code read on an existing file with spaces in its path returns the file contents on the CLI lane (no silent search)
